title: phegopteris incisa
intro: Phegopteris incisa, also known as the cut-leaved polypody or cut-leaf polypody, is a species of epiphytic fern native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. It is believed to have several health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. Phegopteris incisa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts for medicinal use, although its availability and usage may vary in different regions.
